D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Lead White — From Vermeer's Glow to Modern Ban

2 PbCO₃·Pb(OH)₂ — the only opaque white from Roman times to 1900; high refractive index gives Vermeer's luminous flesh tones, but toxicity drove its modern phase-out

Lead white made by stacking lead sheets over vinegar in dung beds (the Dutch "stack process"). RI ≈ 2.0 → exceptional opacity in oil. Painters wore gloves and still developed "painter's colic" — lead poisoning. EU restricted artist use in 2018; restoration still requires it for matching original works.

Toggle three white pigments: lead, zinc, titanium.
